<a name="CopyFS"></a>

## func [CopyFS](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/dir.go#L145>)

```go
func CopyFS(dir string, fsys fs.FS) error
```

CopyFS copies the file system fsys into the directory dir, creating dir if necessary.

Files are created with mode 0o666 plus any execute permissions from the source, and directories are created with mode 0o777 \(before umask\).

CopyFS will not overwrite existing files. If a file name in fsys already exists in the destination, CopyFS will return an error such that errors.Is\(err, fs.ErrExist\) will be true.

Symbolic links in dir are followed.

New files added to fsys \(including if dir is a subdirectory of fsys\) while CopyFS is running are not guaranteed to be copied.

Copying stops at and returns the first error encountered.

<a name="DirFS"></a>

## func [DirFS](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L743>)

```go
func DirFS(dir string) fs.FS
```

DirFS returns a file system \(an fs.FS\) for the tree of files rooted at the directory dir.

Note that DirFS\("/prefix"\) only guarantees that the Open calls it makes to the operating system will begin with "/prefix": DirFS\("/prefix"\).Open\("file"\) is the same as os.Open\("/prefix/file"\). So if /prefix/file is a symbolic link pointing outside the /prefix tree, then using DirFS does not stop the access any more than using os.Open does. Additionally, the root of the fs.FS returned for a relative path, DirFS\("prefix"\), will be affected by later calls to Chdir. DirFS is therefore not a general substitute for a chroot\-style security mechanism when the directory tree contains arbitrary content.

Use [Root.FS](<#Root.FS>) to obtain a fs.FS that prevents escapes from the tree via symbolic links.

The directory dir must not be "".

The result implements [io/fs.StatFS](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/fs/#StatFS>), [io/fs.ReadFileFS](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/fs/#ReadFileFS>), [io/fs.ReadDirFS](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/fs/#ReadDirFS>), and [io/fs.ReadLinkFS](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/fs/#ReadLinkFS>).

<a name="Environ"></a>

## func [Environ](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/env.go#L139>)

```go
func Environ() []string
```

Environ returns a copy of strings representing the environment, in the form "key=value".

<a name="Executable"></a>

## func [Executable](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/executable.go#L18>)

```go
func Executable() (string, error)
```

Executable returns the path name for the executable that started the current process. There is no guarantee that the path is still pointing to the correct executable. If a symlink was used to start the process, depending on the operating system, the result might be the symlink or the path it pointed to. If a stable result is needed, [path/filepath.EvalSymlinks](<https://pkg.go.dev/path/filepath/#EvalSymlinks>) might help.

Executable returns an absolute path unless an error occurred.

The main use case is finding resources located relative to an executable.

<a name="Exit"></a>

## func [Exit](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/proc.go#L62>)

```go
func Exit(code int)
```

Exit causes the current program to exit with the given status code. Conventionally, code zero indicates success, non\-zero an error. The program terminates immediately; deferred functions are not run.

For portability, the status code should be in the range \[0, 125\].

<a name="Expand"></a>

## func [Expand](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/env.go#L16>)

```go
func Expand(s string, mapping func(string) string) string
```

Expand replaces $\{var\} or $var in the string based on the mapping function. For example, [os.ExpandEnv](<#ExpandEnv>)\(s\) is equivalent to [os.Expand](<#Expand>)\(s, [os.Getenv](<#Getenv>)\).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	mapper := func(placeholderName string) string {
		switch placeholderName {
		case "DAY_PART":
			return "morning"
		case "NAME":
			return "Gopher"
		}

		return ""
	}

	fmt.Println(os.Expand("Good ${DAY_PART}, $NAME!", mapper))

}
```

#### Output

```go
Good morning, Gopher!
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ExpandEnv"></a>

## func [ExpandEnv](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/env.go#L50>)

```go
func ExpandEnv(s string) string
```

ExpandEnv replaces $\{var\} or $var in the string according to the values of the current environment variables. References to undefined variables are replaced by the empty string.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	os.Setenv("NAME", "gopher")
	os.Setenv("BURROW", "/usr/gopher")

	fmt.Println(os.ExpandEnv("$NAME lives in ${BURROW}."))

}
```

#### Output

```go
gopher lives in /usr/gopher.
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Getegid"></a>

## func [Getegid](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/proc.go#L46>)

```go
func Getegid() int
```

Getegid returns the numeric effective group id of the caller.

On Windows, it returns \-1.

<a name="Getenv"></a>

## func [Getenv](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/env.go#L101>)

```go
func Getenv(key string) string
```

Getenv retrieves the value of the environment variable named by the key. It returns the value, which will be empty if the variable is not present. To distinguish between an empty value and an unset value, use [LookupEnv](<#LookupEnv>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	os.Setenv("NAME", "gopher")
	os.Setenv("BURROW", "/usr/gopher")

	fmt.Printf("%s lives in %s.\n", os.Getenv("NAME"), os.Getenv("BURROW"))

}
```

#### Output

```go
gopher lives in /usr/gopher.
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Geteuid"></a>

## func [Geteuid](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/proc.go#L36>)

```go
func Geteuid() int
```

Geteuid returns the numeric effective user id of the caller.

On Windows, it returns \-1.

<a name="Getgid"></a>

## func [Getgid](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/proc.go#L41>)

```go
func Getgid() int
```

Getgid returns the numeric group id of the caller.

On Windows, it returns \-1.

<a name="Getgroups"></a>

## func [Getgroups](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/proc.go#L52>)

```go
func Getgroups() ([]int, error)
```

Getgroups returns a list of the numeric ids of groups that the caller belongs to.

On Windows, it returns [syscall.EWINDOWS](<https://pkg.go.dev/syscall/#EWINDOWS>). See the [os/user](<https://pkg.go.dev/os/user/>) package for a possible alternative.

<a name="Getpagesize"></a>

## func [Getpagesize](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/types.go#L13>)

```go
func Getpagesize() int
```

Getpagesize returns the underlying system's memory page size.

<a name="Getpid"></a>

## func [Getpid](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L239>)

```go
func Getpid() int
```

Getpid returns the process id of the caller.

<a name="Getppid"></a>

## func [Getppid](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L242>)

```go
func Getppid() int
```

Getppid returns the process id of the caller's parent.

<a name="Getuid"></a>

## func [Getuid](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/proc.go#L31>)

```go
func Getuid() int
```

Getuid returns the numeric user id of the caller.

On Windows, it returns \-1.

<a name="Getwd"></a>

## func [Getwd](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/getwd.go#L26>)

```go
func Getwd() (dir string, err error)
```

Getwd returns an absolute path name corresponding to the current directory. If the current directory can be reached via multiple paths \(due to symbolic links\), Getwd may return any one of them.

On Unix platforms, if the environment variable PWD provides an absolute name, and it is a name of the current directory, it is returned.

<a name="Hostname"></a>

## func [Hostname](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/sys.go#L8>)

```go
func Hostname() (name string, err error)
```

Hostname returns the host name reported by the kernel.

<a name="IsExist"></a>

## func [IsExist](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/error.go#L80>)

```go
func IsExist(err error) bool
```

IsExist returns a boolean indicating whether its argument is known to report that a file or directory already exists. It is satisfied by [ErrExist](<#ErrInvalid>) as well as some syscall errors.

This function predates [errors.Is](<https://pkg.go.dev/errors/#Is>). It only supports errors returned by the os package. New code should use errors.Is\(err, fs.ErrExist\).

<a name="IsNotExist"></a>

## func [IsNotExist](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/error.go#L90>)

```go
func IsNotExist(err error) bool
```

IsNotExist returns a boolean indicating whether its argument is known to report that a file or directory does not exist. It is satisfied by [ErrNotExist](<#ErrInvalid>) as well as some syscall errors.

This function predates [errors.Is](<https://pkg.go.dev/errors/#Is>). It only supports errors returned by the os package. New code should use errors.Is\(err, fs.ErrNotExist\).

<a name="IsPathSeparator"></a>

## func [IsPathSeparator](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/path_unix.go#L15>)

```go
func IsPathSeparator(c uint8) bool
```

IsPathSeparator reports whether c is a directory separator character.

<a name="IsPermission"></a>

## func [IsPermission](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/error.go#L100>)

```go
func IsPermission(err error) bool
```

IsPermission returns a boolean indicating whether its argument is known to report that permission is denied. It is satisfied by [ErrPermission](<#ErrInvalid>) as well as some syscall errors.

This function predates [errors.Is](<https://pkg.go.dev/errors/#Is>). It only supports errors returned by the os package. New code should use errors.Is\(err, fs.ErrPermission\).

<a name="IsTimeout"></a>

## func [IsTimeout](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/error.go#L112>)

```go
func IsTimeout(err error) bool
```

IsTimeout returns a boolean indicating whether its argument is known to report that a timeout occurred.

This function predates [errors.Is](<https://pkg.go.dev/errors/#Is>), and the notion of whether an error indicates a timeout can be ambiguous. For example, the Unix error EWOULDBLOCK sometimes indicates a timeout and sometimes does not. New code should use errors.Is with a value appropriate to the call returning the error, such as [os.ErrDeadlineExceeded](<#ErrInvalid>).

<a name="Lchown"></a>

## func [Lchown](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file_posix.go#L121>)

```go
func Lchown(name string, uid, gid int) error
```

Lchown changes the numeric uid and gid of the named file. If the file is a symbolic link, it changes the uid and gid of the link itself. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

On Windows, it always returns the [syscall.EWINDOWS](<https://pkg.go.dev/syscall/#EWINDOWS>) error, wrapped in [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="Link"></a>

## func [Link](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file_unix.go#L404>)

```go
func Link(oldname, newname string) error
```

Link creates newname as a hard link to the oldname file. If there is an error, it will be of type \*LinkError.

<a name="LookupEnv"></a>

## func [LookupEnv](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/env.go#L112>)

```go
func LookupEnv(key string) (string, bool)
```

LookupEnv retrieves the value of the environment variable named by the key. If the variable is present in the environment the value \(which may be empty\) is returned and the boolean is true. Otherwise the returned value will be empty and the boolean will be false.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	show := func(key string) {
		val, ok := os.LookupEnv(key)
		if !ok {
			fmt.Printf("%s not set\n", key)
		} else {
			fmt.Printf("%s=%s\n", key, val)
		}
	}

	os.Setenv("SOME_KEY", "value")
	os.Setenv("EMPTY_KEY", "")

	show("SOME_KEY")
	show("EMPTY_KEY")
	show("MISSING_KEY")

}
```

#### Output

```go
SOME_KEY=value
EMPTY_KEY=
MISSING_KEY not set
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Mkdir"></a>

## func [Mkdir](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L327>)

```go
func Mkdir(name string, perm FileMode) error
```

Mkdir creates a new directory with the specified name and permission bits \(before umask\). If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	err := os.Mkdir("testdir", 0750)
	if err != nil && !os.IsExist(err) {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	err = os.WriteFile("testdir/testfile.txt", []byte("Hello, Gophers!"), 0660)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="MkdirAll"></a>

## func [MkdirAll](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/path.go#L19>)

```go
func MkdirAll(path string, perm FileMode) error
```

MkdirAll creates a directory named path, along with any necessary parents, and returns nil, or else returns an error. The permission bits perm \(before umask\) are used for all directories that MkdirAll creates. If path is already a directory, MkdirAll does nothing and returns nil.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	err := os.MkdirAll("test/subdir", 0750)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	err = os.WriteFile("test/subdir/testfile.txt", []byte("Hello, Gophers!"), 0660)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="MkdirTemp"></a>

## func [MkdirTemp](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/tempfile.go#L86>)

```go
func MkdirTemp(dir, pattern string) (string, error)
```

MkdirTemp creates a new temporary directory in the directory dir and returns the pathname of the new directory. The new directory's name is generated by adding a random string to the end of pattern. If pattern includes a "\*", the random string replaces the last "\*" instead. The directory is created with mode 0o700 \(before umask\). If dir is the empty string, MkdirTemp uses the default directory for temporary files, as returned by TempDir. Multiple programs or goroutines calling MkdirTemp simultaneously will not choose the same directory. It is the caller's responsibility to remove the directory when it is no longer needed.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
)

func main() {
	dir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "example")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.RemoveAll(dir) // clean up

	file := filepath.Join(dir, "tmpfile")
	if err := os.WriteFile(file, []byte("content"), 0666); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Suffix)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
)

func main() {
	logsDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "*-logs")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.RemoveAll(logsDir) // clean up

	// Logs can be cleaned out earlier if needed by searching
	// for all directories whose suffix ends in *-logs.
	globPattern := filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), "*-logs")
	matches, err := filepath.Glob(globPattern)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("Failed to match %q: %v", globPattern, err)
	}

	for _, match := range matches {
		if err := os.RemoveAll(match); err != nil {
			log.Printf("Failed to remove %q: %v", match, err)
		}
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewSyscallError"></a>

## func [NewSyscallError](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/error.go#L67>)

```go
func NewSyscallError(syscall string, err error) error
```

NewSyscallError returns, as an error, a new [SyscallError](<#SyscallError>) with the given system call name and error details. As a convenience, if err is nil, NewSyscallError returns nil.

<a name="Pipe"></a>

## func [Pipe](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/pipe_unix.go#L13>)

```go
func Pipe() (r *File, w *File, err error)
```

Pipe returns a connected pair of Files; reads from r return bytes written to w. It returns the files and an error, if any.

<a name="ReadFile"></a>

## func [ReadFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L864>)

```go
func ReadFile(name string) ([]byte, error)
```

ReadFile reads the named file and returns the contents. A successful call returns err == nil, not err == EOF. Because ReadFile reads the whole file, it does not treat an EOF from Read as an error to be reported.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	data, err := os.ReadFile("testdata/hello")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	os.Stdout.Write(data)

}
```

#### Output

```go
Hello, Gophers!
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Readlink"></a>

## func [Readlink](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L446>)

```go
func Readlink(name string) (string, error)
```

Readlink returns the destination of the named symbolic link. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

If the link destination is relative, Readlink returns the relative path without resolving it to an absolute one.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
)

func main() {
	// First, we create a relative symlink to a file.
	d, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.RemoveAll(d)
	targetPath := filepath.Join(d, "hello.txt")
	if err := os.WriteFile(targetPath, []byte("Hello, Gophers!"), 0644); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	linkPath := filepath.Join(d, "hello.link")
	if err := os.Symlink("hello.txt", filepath.Join(d, "hello.link")); err != nil {
		if errors.Is(err, errors.ErrUnsupported) {
			// Allow the example to run on platforms that do not support symbolic links.
			fmt.Printf("%s links to %s\n", filepath.Base(linkPath), "hello.txt")
			return
		}
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	// Readlink returns the relative path as passed to os.Symlink.
	dst, err := os.Readlink(linkPath)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	fmt.Printf("%s links to %s\n", filepath.Base(linkPath), dst)

	var dstAbs string
	if filepath.IsAbs(dst) {
		dstAbs = dst
	} else {
		// Symlink targets are relative to the directory containing the link.
		dstAbs = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(linkPath), dst)
	}

	// Check that the target is correct by comparing it with os.Stat
	// on the original target path.
	dstInfo, err := os.Stat(dstAbs)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	targetInfo, err := os.Stat(targetPath)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	if !os.SameFile(dstInfo, targetInfo) {
		log.Fatalf("link destination (%s) is not the same file as %s", dstAbs, targetPath)
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go
hello.link links to hello.txt
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Remove"></a>

## func [Remove](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file_unix.go#L357>)

```go
func Remove(name string) error
```

Remove removes the named file or \(empty\) directory. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="RemoveAll"></a>

## func [RemoveAll](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/path.go#L73>)

```go
func RemoveAll(path string) error
```

RemoveAll removes path and any children it contains. It removes everything it can but returns the first error it encounters. If the path does not exist, RemoveAll returns nil \(no error\). If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="Rename"></a>

## func [Rename](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L437>)

```go
func Rename(oldpath, newpath string) error
```

Rename renames \(moves\) oldpath to newpath. If newpath already exists and is not a directory, Rename replaces it. If newpath already exists and is a directory, Rename returns an error. OS\-specific restrictions may apply when oldpath and newpath are in different directories. Even within the same directory, on non\-Unix platforms Rename is not an atomic operation. If there is an error, it will be of type \*LinkError.

<a name="SameFile"></a>

## func [SameFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/types.go#L69>)

```go
func SameFile(fi1, fi2 FileInfo) bool
```

SameFile reports whether fi1 and fi2 describe the same file. For example, on Unix this means that the device and inode fields of the two underlying structures are identical; on other systems the decision may be based on the path names. SameFile only applies to results returned by this package's [Stat](<#Stat>). It returns false in other cases.

<a name="Setenv"></a>

## func [Setenv](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/env.go#L119>)

```go
func Setenv(key, value string) error
```

Setenv sets the value of the environment variable named by the key. It returns an error, if any.

<a name="Symlink"></a>

## func [Symlink](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file_unix.go#L418>)

```go
func Symlink(oldname, newname string) error
```

Symlink creates newname as a symbolic link to oldname. On Windows, a symlink to a non\-existent oldname creates a file symlink; if oldname is later created as a directory the symlink will not work. If there is an error, it will be of type \*LinkError.

<a name="TempDir"></a>

## func [TempDir](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L487>)

```go
func TempDir() string
```

TempDir returns the default directory to use for temporary files.

On Unix systems, it returns $TMPDIR if non\-empty, else /tmp. On Windows, it uses GetTempPath, returning the first non\-empty value from %TMP%, %TEMP%, %USERPROFILE%, or the Windows directory. On Plan 9, it returns /tmp.

The directory is neither guaranteed to exist nor have accessible permissions.

<a name="Truncate"></a>

## func [Truncate](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file_unix.go#L345>)

```go
func Truncate(name string, size int64) error
```

Truncate changes the size of the named file. If the file is a symbolic link, it changes the size of the link's target. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="Unsetenv"></a>

## func [Unsetenv](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/env.go#L128>)

```go
func Unsetenv(key string) error
```

Unsetenv unsets a single environment variable.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"os"
)

func main() {
	os.Setenv("TMPDIR", "/my/tmp")
	defer os.Unsetenv("TMPDIR")
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="UserCacheDir"></a>

## func [UserCacheDir](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L504>)

```go
func UserCacheDir() (string, error)
```

UserCacheDir returns the default root directory to use for user\-specific cached data. Users should create their own application\-specific subdirectory within this one and use that.

On Unix systems, it returns $XDG\_CACHE\_HOME as specified by https://specifications.freedesktop.org/basedir-spec/basedir-spec-latest.html if non\-empty, else $HOME/.cache. On Darwin, it returns $HOME/Library/Caches. On Windows, it returns %LocalAppData%. On Plan 9, it returns $home/lib/cache.

If the location cannot be determined \(for example, $HOME is not defined\) or the path in $XDG\_CACHE\_HOME is relative, then it will return an error.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"sync"
)

func main() {
	dir, dirErr := os.UserCacheDir()
	if dirErr == nil {
		dir = filepath.Join(dir, "ExampleUserCacheDir")
	}

	getCache := func(name string) ([]byte, error) {
		if dirErr != nil {
			return nil, &os.PathError{Op: "getCache", Path: name, Err: os.ErrNotExist}
		}
		return os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, name))
	}

	var mkdirOnce sync.Once
	putCache := func(name string, b []byte) error {
		if dirErr != nil {
			return &os.PathError{Op: "putCache", Path: name, Err: dirErr}
		}
		mkdirOnce.Do(func() {
			if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0700); err != nil {
				log.Printf("can't create user cache dir: %v", err)
			}
		})
		return os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, name), b, 0600)
	}

	// Read and store cached data.
	// …
	_ = getCache
	_ = putCache

}
```

#### Output

```go

```

</p>
</details>

<a name="UserConfigDir"></a>

## func [UserConfigDir](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L557>)

```go
func UserConfigDir() (string, error)
```

UserConfigDir returns the default root directory to use for user\-specific configuration data. Users should create their own application\-specific subdirectory within this one and use that.

On Unix systems, it returns $XDG\_CONFIG\_HOME as specified by https://specifications.freedesktop.org/basedir-spec/basedir-spec-latest.html if non\-empty, else $HOME/.config. On Darwin, it returns $HOME/Library/Application Support. On Windows, it returns %AppData%. On Plan 9, it returns $home/lib.

If the location cannot be determined \(for example, $HOME is not defined\) or the path in $XDG\_CONFIG\_HOME is relative, then it will return an error.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"log"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
)

func main() {
	dir, dirErr := os.UserConfigDir()

	var (
		configPath string
		origConfig []byte
	)
	if dirErr == nil {
		configPath = filepath.Join(dir, "ExampleUserConfigDir", "example.conf")
		var err error
		origConfig, err = os.ReadFile(configPath)
		if err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
			// The user has a config file but we couldn't read it.
			// Report the error instead of ignoring their configuration.
			log.Fatal(err)
		}
	}

	// Use and perhaps make changes to the config.
	config := bytes.Clone(origConfig)
	// …

	// Save changes.
	if !bytes.Equal(config, origConfig) {
		if configPath == "" {
			log.Printf("not saving config changes: %v", dirErr)
		} else {
			err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(configPath), 0700)
			if err == nil {
				err = os.WriteFile(configPath, config, 0600)
			}
			if err != nil {
				log.Printf("error saving config changes: %v", err)
			}
		}
	}

}
```

#### Output

```go

```

</p>
</details>

<a name="UserHomeDir"></a>

## func [UserHomeDir](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L605>)

```go
func UserHomeDir() (string, error)
```

UserHomeDir returns the current user's home directory.

On Unix, including macOS, it returns the $HOME environment variable. On Windows, it returns %USERPROFILE%. On Plan 9, it returns the $home environment variable.

If the expected variable is not set in the environment, UserHomeDir returns either a platform\-specific default value or a non\-nil error.

<a name="WriteFile"></a>

## func [WriteFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L932>)

```go
func WriteFile(name string, data []byte, perm FileMode) error
```

WriteFile writes data to the named file, creating it if necessary. If the file does not exist, WriteFile creates it with permissions perm \(before umask\); otherwise WriteFile truncates it before writing, without changing permissions. Since WriteFile requires multiple system calls to complete, a failure mid\-operation can leave the file in a partially written state.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	err := os.WriteFile("testdata/hello", []byte("Hello, Gophers!"), 0666)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="DirEntry"></a>

## type [DirEntry](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/dir.go#L85>)

A DirEntry is an entry read from a directory \(using the [ReadDir](<#ReadDir>) function or a [File.ReadDir](<#File.ReadDir>) method\).

```go
type DirEntry = fs.DirEntry
```

<a name="ReadDir"></a>

### func [ReadDir](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/dir.go#L114>)

```go
func ReadDir(name string) ([]DirEntry, error)
```

ReadDir reads the named directory, returning all its directory entries sorted by filename. If an error occurs reading the directory, ReadDir returns the entries it was able to read before the error, along with the error.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	files, err := os.ReadDir(".")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	for _, file := range files {
		fmt.Println(file.Name())
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="File"></a>

## type [File](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/types.go#L18-L20>)

File represents an open file descriptor.

The methods of File are safe for concurrent use.

```go
type File struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Create"></a>

### func [Create](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L399>)

```go
func Create(name string) (*File, error)
```

Create creates or truncates the named file. If the file already exists, it is truncated. If the file does not exist, it is created with mode 0o666 \(before umask\). If successful, methods on the returned File can be used for I/O; the associated file descriptor has mode [O\\\_RDWR](<#O_RDONLY>). The directory containing the file must already exist. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="CreateTemp"></a>

### func [CreateTemp](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/tempfile.go#L35>)

```go
func CreateTemp(dir, pattern string) (*File, error)
```

CreateTemp creates a new temporary file in the directory dir, opens the file for reading and writing, and returns the resulting file. The filename is generated by taking pattern and adding a random string to the end. If pattern includes a "\*", the random string replaces the last "\*". The file is created with mode 0o600 \(before umask\). If dir is the empty string, CreateTemp uses the default directory for temporary files, as returned by [TempDir](<#TempDir>). Multiple programs or goroutines calling CreateTemp simultaneously will not choose the same file. The caller can use the file's Name method to find the pathname of the file. It is the caller's responsibility to remove the file when it is no longer needed.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	f, err := os.CreateTemp("", "example")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.Remove(f.Name()) // clean up

	if _, err := f.Write([]byte("content")); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Suffix)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	f, err := os.CreateTemp("", "example.*.txt")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer os.Remove(f.Name()) // clean up

	if _, err := f.Write([]byte("content")); err != nil {
		f.Close()
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewFile"></a>

### func [NewFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L133>)

```go
func NewFile(fd uintptr, name string) *File
```

NewFile returns a new [File](<#File>) with the given file descriptor and name. The returned value will be nil if fd is not a valid file descriptor.

NewFile's behavior differs on some platforms:

- On Unix, if fd is in non\-blocking mode, NewFile will attempt to return a pollable file.
- On Windows, if fd is opened for asynchronous I/O \(that is, [syscall.FILE\\\_FLAG\\\_OVERLAPPED](<https://pkg.go.dev/syscall/#FILE_FLAG_OVERLAPPED>) has been specified in the [syscall.CreateFile](<https://pkg.go.dev/syscall/#CreateFile>) call\), NewFile will attempt to return a pollable file by associating fd with the Go runtime I/O completion port. The I/O operations will be performed synchronously if the association fails.

Only pollable files support [File.SetDeadline](<#File.SetDeadline>), [File.SetReadDeadline](<#File.SetReadDeadline>), and [File.SetWriteDeadline](<#File.SetWriteDeadline>).

After passing it to NewFile, fd may become invalid under the same conditions described in the comments of [File.Fd](<#File.Fd>), and the same constraints apply.

<a name="Open"></a>

### func [Open](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L389>)

```go
func Open(name string) (*File, error)
```

Open opens the named file for reading. If successful, methods on the returned file can be used for reading; the associated file descriptor has mode [O\\\_RDONLY](<#O_RDONLY>). If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="OpenFile"></a>

### func [OpenFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L410>)

```go
func OpenFile(name string, flag int, perm FileMode) (*File, error)
```

OpenFile is the generalized open call; most users will use Open or Create instead. It opens the named file with specified flag \([O\\\_RDONLY](<#O_RDONLY>) etc.\). If the file does not exist, and the [O\\\_CREATE](<#O_RDONLY>) flag is passed, it is created with mode perm \(before umask\); the containing directory must exist. If successful, methods on the returned File can be used for I/O. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	f, err := os.OpenFile("notes.txt", os.O_RDWR|os.O_CREATE, 0644)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Append)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	// If the file doesn't exist, create it, or append to the file
	f, err := os.OpenFile("access.log", os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0644)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	if _, err := f.Write([]byte("appended some data\n")); err != nil {
		f.Close() // ignore error; Write error takes precedence
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="OpenInRoot"></a>

### func [OpenInRoot](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L25>)

```go
func OpenInRoot(dir, name string) (*File, error)
```

OpenInRoot opens the file name in the directory dir. It is equivalent to OpenRoot\(dir\) followed by opening the file in the root.

OpenInRoot returns an error if any component of the name references a location outside of dir.

See [Root](<#Root>) for details and limitations.

<a name="File.Chdir"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Chdir](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file_posix.go#L204>)

```go
func (f *File) Chdir() error
```

Chdir changes the current working directory to the file, which must be a directory. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="File.Chmod"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Chmod](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L648>)

```go
func (f *File) Chmod(mode FileMode) error
```

Chmod changes the mode of the file to mode. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="File.Chown"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Chown](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file_posix.go#L136>)

```go
func (f *File) Chown(uid, gid int) error
```

Chown changes the numeric uid and gid of the named file. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

On Windows, it always returns the [syscall.EWINDOWS](<https://pkg.go.dev/syscall/#EWINDOWS>) error, wrapped in [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="File.Close"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Close](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file_posix.go#L19>)

```go
func (f *File) Close() error
```

Close closes the [File](<#File>), rendering it unusable for I/O. On files that support [File.SetDeadline](<#File.SetDeadline>), any pending I/O operations will be canceled and return immediately with an [ErrClosed](<#ErrInvalid>) error. Close will return an error if it has already been called.

<a name="File.Fd"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Fd](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L722>)

```go
func (f *File) Fd() uintptr
```

Fd returns the system file descriptor or handle referencing the open file. If f is closed, the descriptor becomes invalid. If f is garbage collected, a finalizer may close the descriptor, making it invalid; see [runtime.SetFinalizer](<https://pkg.go.dev/runtime/#SetFinalizer>) for more information on when a finalizer might be run.

Do not close the returned descriptor; that could cause a later close of f to close an unrelated descriptor.

Fd's behavior differs on some platforms:

- On Unix and Windows, [File.SetDeadline](<#File.SetDeadline>) methods will stop working.
- On Windows, the file descriptor will be disassociated from the Go runtime I/O completion port if there are no concurrent I/O operations on the file.

For most uses prefer the f.SyscallConn method.

<a name="File.Name"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Name](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L63>)

```go
func (f *File) Name() string
```

Name returns the name of the file as presented to Open.

It is safe to call Name after \[Close\].

<a name="File.Read"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Read](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L140>)

```go
func (f *File) Read(b []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Read reads up to len\(b\) bytes from the File and stores them in b. It returns the number of bytes read and any error encountered. At end of file, Read returns 0, io.EOF.

<a name="File.ReadAt"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [ReadAt](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L152>)

```go
func (f *File) ReadAt(b []byte, off int64) (n int, err error)
```

ReadAt reads len\(b\) bytes from the File starting at byte offset off. It returns the number of bytes read and the error, if any. ReadAt always returns a non\-nil error when n \< len\(b\). At end of file, that error is io.EOF.

<a name="File.ReadDir"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [ReadDir](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/dir.go#L97>)

```go
func (f *File) ReadDir(n int) ([]DirEntry, error)
```

ReadDir reads the contents of the directory associated with the file f and returns a slice of [DirEntry](<#DirEntry>) values in directory order. Subsequent calls on the same file will yield later DirEntry records in the directory.

If n \> 0, ReadDir returns at most n DirEntry records. In this case, if ReadDir returns an empty slice, it will return an error explaining why. At the end of a directory, the error is [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>).

If n \<= 0, ReadDir returns all the DirEntry records remaining in the directory. When it succeeds, it returns a nil error \(not io.EOF\).

<a name="File.ReadFrom"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [ReadFrom](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L175>)

```go
func (f *File) ReadFrom(r io.Reader) (n int64, err error)
```

ReadFrom implements io.ReaderFrom.

<a name="File.Readdir"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Readdir](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/dir.go#L40>)

```go
func (f *File) Readdir(n int) ([]FileInfo, error)
```

Readdir reads the contents of the directory associated with file and returns a slice of up to n [FileInfo](<#FileInfo>) values, as would be returned by [Lstat](<#Lstat>), in directory order. Subsequent calls on the same file will yield further FileInfos.

If n \> 0, Readdir returns at most n FileInfo structures. In this case, if Readdir returns an empty slice, it will return a non\-nil error explaining why. At the end of a directory, the error is [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>).

If n \<= 0, Readdir returns all the FileInfo from the directory in a single slice. In this case, if Readdir succeeds \(reads all the way to the end of the directory\), it returns the slice and a nil error. If it encounters an error before the end of the directory, Readdir returns the FileInfo read until that point and a non\-nil error.

Most clients are better served by the more efficient ReadDir method.

<a name="File.Readdirnames"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Readdirnames](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/dir.go#L69>)

```go
func (f *File) Readdirnames(n int) (names []string, err error)
```

Readdirnames reads the contents of the directory associated with file and returns a slice of up to n names of files in the directory, in directory order. Subsequent calls on the same file will yield further names.

If n \> 0, Readdirnames returns at most n names. In this case, if Readdirnames returns an empty slice, it will return a non\-nil error explaining why. At the end of a directory, the error is [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>).

If n \<= 0, Readdirnames returns all the names from the directory in a single slice. In this case, if Readdirnames succeeds \(reads all the way to the end of the directory\), it returns the slice and a nil error. If it encounters an error before the end of the directory, Readdirnames returns the names read until that point and a non\-nil error.

<a name="File.Seek"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Seek](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L303>)

```go
func (f *File) Seek(offset int64, whence int) (ret int64, err error)
```

Seek sets the offset for the next Read or Write on file to offset, interpreted according to whence: 0 means relative to the origin of the file, 1 means relative to the current offset, and 2 means relative to the end. It returns the new offset and an error, if any. The behavior of Seek on a file opened with [O\\\_APPEND](<#O_RDONLY>) is not specified.

<a name="File.SetDeadline"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [SetDeadline](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L674>)

```go
func (f *File) SetDeadline(t time.Time) error
```

SetDeadline sets the read and write deadlines for a File. It is equivalent to calling both SetReadDeadline and SetWriteDeadline.

Only some kinds of files support setting a deadline. Calls to SetDeadline for files that do not support deadlines will return ErrNoDeadline. On most systems ordinary files do not support deadlines, but pipes do.

A deadline is an absolute time after which I/O operations fail with an error instead of blocking. The deadline applies to all future and pending I/O, not just the immediately following call to Read or Write. After a deadline has been exceeded, the connection can be refreshed by setting a deadline in the future.

If the deadline is exceeded a call to Read or Write or to other I/O methods will return an error that wraps ErrDeadlineExceeded. This can be tested using errors.Is\(err, os.ErrDeadlineExceeded\). That error implements the Timeout method, and calling the Timeout method will return true, but there are other possible errors for which the Timeout will return true even if the deadline has not been exceeded.

An idle timeout can be implemented by repeatedly extending the deadline after successful Read or Write calls.

A zero value for t means I/O operations will not time out.

<a name="File.SetReadDeadline"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [SetReadDeadline](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L682>)

```go
func (f *File) SetReadDeadline(t time.Time) error
```

SetReadDeadline sets the deadline for future Read calls and any currently\-blocked Read call. A zero value for t means Read will not time out. Not all files support setting deadlines; see SetDeadline.

<a name="File.SetWriteDeadline"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [SetWriteDeadline](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L692>)

```go
func (f *File) SetWriteDeadline(t time.Time) error
```

SetWriteDeadline sets the deadline for any future Write calls and any currently\-blocked Write call. Even if Write times out, it may return n \> 0, indicating that some of the data was successfully written. A zero value for t means Write will not time out. Not all files support setting deadlines; see SetDeadline.

<a name="File.Stat"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Stat](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/stat_unix.go#L15>)

```go
func (f *File) Stat() (FileInfo, error)
```

Stat returns the [FileInfo](<#FileInfo>) structure describing file. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="File.Sync"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Sync](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file_posix.go#L162>)

```go
func (f *File) Sync() error
```

Sync commits the current contents of the file to stable storage. Typically, this means flushing the file system's in\-memory copy of recently written data to disk.

<a name="File.SyscallConn"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [SyscallConn](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L698>)

```go
func (f *File) SyscallConn() (syscall.RawConn, error)
```

SyscallConn returns a raw file. This implements the syscall.Conn interface.

<a name="File.Truncate"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Truncate](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file_posix.go#L149>)

```go
func (f *File) Truncate(size int64) error
```

Truncate changes the size of the file. It does not change the I/O offset. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="File.Write"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [Write](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L211>)

```go
func (f *File) Write(b []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Write writes len\(b\) bytes from b to the File. It returns the number of bytes written and an error, if any. Write returns a non\-nil error when n \!= len\(b\).

<a name="File.WriteAt"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [WriteAt](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L239>)

```go
func (f *File) WriteAt(b []byte, off int64) (n int, err error)
```

WriteAt writes len\(b\) bytes to the File starting at byte offset off. It returns the number of bytes written and an error, if any. WriteAt returns a non\-nil error when n \!= len\(b\).

If file was opened with the [O\\\_APPEND](<#O_RDONLY>) flag, WriteAt returns an error.

<a name="File.WriteString"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [WriteString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L319>)

```go
func (f *File) WriteString(s string) (n int, err error)
```

WriteString is like Write, but writes the contents of string s rather than a slice of bytes.

<a name="File.WriteTo"></a>

### func \(\*File\) [WriteTo](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L265>)

```go
func (f *File) WriteTo(w io.Writer) (n int64, err error)
```

WriteTo implements io.WriterTo.

<a name="FileInfo"></a>

## type [FileInfo](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/types.go#L23>)

A FileInfo describes a file and is returned by [Stat](<#Stat>) and [Lstat](<#Lstat>).

```go
type FileInfo = fs.FileInfo
```

<a name="Lstat"></a>

### func [Lstat](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/stat.go#L24>)

```go
func Lstat(name string) (FileInfo, error)
```

Lstat returns a [FileInfo](<#FileInfo>) describing the named file. If the file is a symbolic link, the returned FileInfo describes the symbolic link. Lstat makes no attempt to follow the link. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

On Windows, if the file is a reparse point that is a surrogate for another named entity \(such as a symbolic link or mounted folder\), the returned FileInfo describes the reparse point, and makes no attempt to resolve it.

<a name="Stat"></a>

### func [Stat](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/stat.go#L11>)

```go
func Stat(name string) (FileInfo, error)
```

Stat returns a [FileInfo](<#FileInfo>) describing the named file. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="FileMode"></a>

## type [FileMode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/types.go#L30>)

A FileMode represents a file's mode and permission bits. The bits have the same definition on all systems, so that information about files can be moved from one system to another portably. Not all bits apply to all systems. The only required bit is [ModeDir](<#ModeDir>) for directories.

```go
type FileMode = fs.FileMode
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"io/fs"
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	fi, err := os.Lstat("some-filename")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	fmt.Printf("permissions: %#o\n", fi.Mode().Perm()) // 0o400, 0o777, etc.
	switch mode := fi.Mode(); {
	case mode.IsRegular():
		fmt.Println("regular file")
	case mode.IsDir():
		fmt.Println("directory")
	case mode&fs.ModeSymlink != 0:
		fmt.Println("symbolic link")
	case mode&fs.ModeNamedPipe != 0:
		fmt.Println("named pipe")
	}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="LinkError"></a>

## type [LinkError](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L103-L108>)

LinkError records an error during a link or symlink or rename system call and the paths that caused it.

```go
type LinkError struct {
    Op  string
    Old string
    New string
    Err error
}
```

<a name="LinkError.Error"></a>

### func \(\*LinkError\) [Error](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L110>)

```go
func (e *LinkError) Error() string
```

<a name="LinkError.Unwrap"></a>

### func \(\*LinkError\) [Unwrap](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/file.go#L114>)

```go
func (e *LinkError) Unwrap() error
```

<a name="PathError"></a>

## type [PathError](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/error.go#L46>)

PathError records an error and the operation and file path that caused it.

```go
type PathError = fs.PathError
```

<a name="ProcAttr"></a>

## type [ProcAttr](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L205-L228>)

ProcAttr holds the attributes that will be applied to a new process started by StartProcess.

```go
type ProcAttr struct {
    // If Dir is non-empty, the child changes into the directory before
    // creating the process.
    Dir string
    // If Env is non-nil, it gives the environment variables for the
    // new process in the form returned by Environ.
    // If it is nil, the result of Environ will be used.
    Env []string
    // Files specifies the open files inherited by the new process. The
    // first three entries correspond to standard input, standard output, and
    // standard error. An implementation may support additional entries,
    // depending on the underlying operating system. A nil entry corresponds
    // to that file being closed when the process starts.
    // On Unix systems, StartProcess will change these File values
    // to blocking mode, which means that SetDeadline will stop working
    // and calling Close will not interrupt a Read or Write.
    Files []*File

    // Operating system-specific process creation attributes.
    // Note that setting this field means that your program
    // may not execute properly or even compile on some
    // operating systems.
    Sys *syscall.SysProcAttr
}
```

<a name="Process"></a>

## type [Process](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L43-L65>)

Process stores the information about a process created by [StartProcess](<#StartProcess>).

```go
type Process struct {
    Pid int
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="FindProcess"></a>

### func [FindProcess](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L253>)

```go
func FindProcess(pid int) (*Process, error)
```

FindProcess looks for a running process by its pid.

The [Process](<#Process>) it returns can be used to obtain information about the underlying operating system process.

On Unix systems, FindProcess always succeeds and returns a Process for the given pid, regardless of whether the process exists. To test whether the process actually exists, see whether p.Signal\(syscall.Signal\(0\)\) reports an error.

<a name="StartProcess"></a>

### func [StartProcess](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L270>)

```go
func StartProcess(name string, argv []string, attr *ProcAttr) (*Process, error)
```

StartProcess starts a new process with the program, arguments and attributes specified by name, argv and attr. The argv slice will become [os.Args](<#Args>) in the new process, so it normally starts with the program name.

If the calling goroutine has locked the operating system thread with [runtime.LockOSThread](<https://pkg.go.dev/runtime/#LockOSThread>) and modified any inheritable OS\-level thread state \(for example, Linux or Plan 9 name spaces\), the new process will inherit the caller's thread state.

StartProcess is a low\-level interface. The [os/exec](<https://pkg.go.dev/os/exec/>) package provides higher\-level interfaces.

If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="Process.Kill"></a>

### func \(\*Process\) [Kill](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L336>)

```go
func (p *Process) Kill() error
```

Kill causes the [Process](<#Process>) to exit immediately. Kill does not wait until the Process has actually exited. This only kills the Process itself, not any other processes it may have started.

<a name="Process.Release"></a>

### func \(\*Process\) [Release](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L278>)

```go
func (p *Process) Release() error
```

Release releases any resources associated with the [Process](<#Process>) p, rendering it unusable in the future. Release only needs to be called if [Process.Wait](<#Process.Wait>) is not.

<a name="Process.Signal"></a>

### func \(\*Process\) [Signal](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L351>)

```go
func (p *Process) Signal(sig Signal) error
```

Signal sends a signal to the [Process](<#Process>). Sending [Interrupt](<#Interrupt>) on Windows is not implemented.

<a name="Process.Wait"></a>

### func \(\*Process\) [Wait](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L345>)

```go
func (p *Process) Wait() (*ProcessState, error)
```

Wait waits for the [Process](<#Process>) to exit, and then returns a ProcessState describing its status and an error, if any. Wait releases any resources associated with the Process. On most operating systems, the Process must be a child of the current process or an error will be returned.

<a name="Process.WithHandle"></a>

### func \(\*Process\) [WithHandle](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L363>)

```go
func (p *Process) WithHandle(f func(handle uintptr)) error
```

WithHandle calls a supplied function f with a valid process handle as an argument. The handle is guaranteed to refer to process p until f returns, even if p terminates. This function cannot be used after [Process.Release](<#Process.Release>) or [Process.Wait](<#Process.Wait>).

If process handles are not supported or a handle is not available, it returns [ErrNoHandle](<#ErrProcessDone>). Currently, process handles are supported on Linux 5.4 or later \(pidfd\) and Windows.

<a name="ProcessState"></a>

## type [ProcessState](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec_posix.go#L98-L102>)

ProcessState stores information about a process, as reported by Wait.

```go
type ProcessState struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="ProcessState.ExitCode"></a>

### func \(\*ProcessState\) [ExitCode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec_posix.go#L157>)

```go
func (p *ProcessState) ExitCode() int
```

ExitCode returns the exit code of the exited process, or \-1 if the process hasn't exited or was terminated by a signal.

<a name="ProcessState.Exited"></a>

### func \(\*ProcessState\) [Exited](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L380>)

```go
func (p *ProcessState) Exited() bool
```

Exited reports whether the program has exited. On Unix systems this reports true if the program exited due to calling exit, but false if the program terminated due to a signal.

<a name="ProcessState.Pid"></a>

### func \(\*ProcessState\) [Pid](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec_posix.go#L105>)

```go
func (p *ProcessState) Pid() int
```

Pid returns the process id of the exited process.

<a name="ProcessState.String"></a>

### func \(\*ProcessState\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec_posix.go#L125>)

```go
func (p *ProcessState) String() string
```

<a name="ProcessState.Success"></a>

### func \(\*ProcessState\) [Success](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L386>)

```go
func (p *ProcessState) Success() bool
```

Success reports whether the program exited successfully, such as with exit status 0 on Unix.

<a name="ProcessState.Sys"></a>

### func \(\*ProcessState\) [Sys](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L393>)

```go
func (p *ProcessState) Sys() any
```

Sys returns system\-dependent exit information about the process. Convert it to the appropriate underlying type, such as [syscall.WaitStatus](<https://pkg.go.dev/syscall/#WaitStatus>) on Unix, to access its contents.

<a name="ProcessState.SysUsage"></a>

### func \(\*ProcessState\) [SysUsage](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L402>)

```go
func (p *ProcessState) SysUsage() any
```

SysUsage returns system\-dependent resource usage information about the exited process. Convert it to the appropriate underlying type, such as [\\\*syscall.Rusage](<https://pkg.go.dev/syscall/#Rusage>) on Unix, to access its contents. \(On Unix, \*syscall.Rusage matches struct rusage as defined in the getrusage\(2\) manual page.\)

<a name="ProcessState.SystemTime"></a>

### func \(\*ProcessState\) [SystemTime](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L373>)

```go
func (p *ProcessState) SystemTime() time.Duration
```

SystemTime returns the system CPU time of the exited process and its children.

<a name="ProcessState.UserTime"></a>

### func \(\*ProcessState\) [UserTime](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L368>)

```go
func (p *ProcessState) UserTime() time.Duration
```

UserTime returns the user CPU time of the exited process and its children.

<a name="Root"></a>

## type [Root](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L68-L70>)

Root may be used to only access files within a single directory tree.

Methods on Root can only access files and directories beneath a root directory. If any component of a file name passed to a method of Root references a location outside the root, the method returns an error. File names may reference the directory itself \(.\).

Methods on Root will follow symbolic links, but symbolic links may not reference a location outside the root. Symbolic links must not be absolute.

Methods on Root do not prohibit traversal of filesystem boundaries, Linux bind mounts, /proc special files, or access to Unix device files.

Methods on Root are safe to be used from multiple goroutines simultaneously.

On most platforms, creating a Root opens a file descriptor or handle referencing the directory. If the directory is moved, methods on Root reference the original directory in its new location.

Root's behavior differs on some platforms:

- When GOOS=windows, file names may not reference Windows reserved device names such as NUL and COM1.
- On Unix, [Root.Chmod](<#Root.Chmod>), [Root.Chown](<#Root.Chown>), and [Root.Chtimes](<#Root.Chtimes>) are vulnerable to a race condition. If the target of the operation is changed from a regular file to a symlink while the operation is in progress, the operation may be performed on the link rather than the link target.
- When GOOS=js, Root is vulnerable to TOCTOU \(time\-of\-check\-time\-of\-use\) attacks in symlink validation, and cannot ensure that operations will not escape the root.
- When GOOS=plan9 or GOOS=js, Root does not track directories across renames. On these platforms, a Root references a directory name, not a file descriptor.
- WASI preview 1 \(GOOS=wasip1\) does not support [Root.Chmod](<#Root.Chmod>).

```go
type Root struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="OpenRoot"></a>

### func [OpenRoot](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L82>)

```go
func OpenRoot(name string) (*Root, error)
```

OpenRoot opens the named directory. It follows symbolic links in the directory name. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="Root.Chmod"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Chmod](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L139>)

```go
func (r *Root) Chmod(name string, mode FileMode) error
```

Chmod changes the mode of the named file in the root to mode. See [Chmod](<#Chmod>) for more details.

<a name="Root.Chown"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Chown](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L170>)

```go
func (r *Root) Chown(name string, uid, gid int) error
```

Chown changes the numeric uid and gid of the named file in the root. See [Chown](<#Chown>) for more details.

<a name="Root.Chtimes"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Chtimes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L182>)

```go
func (r *Root) Chtimes(name string, atime time.Time, mtime time.Time) error
```

Chtimes changes the access and modification times of the named file in the root. See [Chtimes](<#Chtimes>) for more details.

<a name="Root.Close"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Close](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L96>)

```go
func (r *Root) Close() error
```

Close closes the Root. After Close is called, methods on Root return errors.

<a name="Root.Create"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Create](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L108>)

```go
func (r *Root) Create(name string) (*File, error)
```

Create creates or truncates the named file in the root. See [Create](<#Create>) for more details.

<a name="Root.FS"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [FS](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L357>)

```go
func (r *Root) FS() fs.FS
```

FS returns a file system \(an fs.FS\) for the tree of files in the root.

The result implements [io/fs.StatFS](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/fs/#StatFS>), [io/fs.ReadFileFS](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/fs/#ReadFileFS>), [io/fs.ReadDirFS](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/fs/#ReadDirFS>), and [io/fs.ReadLinkFS](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/fs/#ReadLinkFS>).

<a name="Root.Lchown"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Lchown](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L176>)

```go
func (r *Root) Lchown(name string, uid, gid int) error
```

Lchown changes the numeric uid and gid of the named file in the root. See [Lchown](<#Lchown>) for more details.

<a name="Root.Link"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Link](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L235>)

```go
func (r *Root) Link(oldname, newname string) error
```

Link creates newname as a hard link to the oldname file. Both paths are relative to the root. See [Link](<#Link>) for more details.

If oldname is a symbolic link, Link creates new link to oldname and not its target. This behavior may differ from that of [Link](<#Link>) on some platforms.

When GOOS=js, Link returns an error if oldname is a symbolic link.

<a name="Root.Lstat"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Lstat](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L209>)

```go
func (r *Root) Lstat(name string) (FileInfo, error)
```

Lstat returns a [FileInfo](<#FileInfo>) describing the named file in the root. If the file is a symbolic link, the returned FileInfo describes the symbolic link. See [Lstat](<#Lstat>) for more details.

<a name="Root.Mkdir"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Mkdir](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L149>)

```go
func (r *Root) Mkdir(name string, perm FileMode) error
```

Mkdir creates a new directory in the root with the specified name and permission bits \(before umask\). See [Mkdir](<#Mkdir>) for more details.

If perm contains bits other than the nine least\-significant bits \(0o777\), Mkdir returns an error.

<a name="Root.MkdirAll"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [MkdirAll](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L161>)

```go
func (r *Root) MkdirAll(name string, perm FileMode) error
```

MkdirAll creates a new directory in the root, along with any necessary parents. See [MkdirAll](<#MkdirAll>) for more details.

If perm contains bits other than the nine least\-significant bits \(0o777\), MkdirAll returns an error.

<a name="Root.Name"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Name](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L90>)

```go
func (r *Root) Name() string
```

Name returns the name of the directory presented to OpenRoot.

It is safe to call Name after \[Close\].

<a name="Root.Open"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Open](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L102>)

```go
func (r *Root) Open(name string) (*File, error)
```

Open opens the named file in the root for reading. See [Open](<#Open>) for more details.

<a name="Root.OpenFile"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [OpenFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L117>)

```go
func (r *Root) OpenFile(name string, flag int, perm FileMode) (*File, error)
```

OpenFile opens the named file in the root. See [OpenFile](<#OpenFile>) for more details.

If perm contains bits other than the nine least\-significant bits \(0o777\), OpenFile returns an error.

<a name="Root.OpenRoot"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [OpenRoot](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L132>)

```go
func (r *Root) OpenRoot(name string) (*Root, error)
```

OpenRoot opens the named directory in the root. If there is an error, it will be of type [\\\*PathError](<#PathError>).

<a name="Root.ReadFile"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [ReadFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L253>)

```go
func (r *Root) ReadFile(name string) ([]byte, error)
```

ReadFile reads the named file in the root and returns its contents. See [ReadFile](<#ReadFile>) for more details.

<a name="Root.Readlink"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Readlink](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L216>)

```go
func (r *Root) Readlink(name string) (string, error)
```

Readlink returns the destination of the named symbolic link in the root. See [Readlink](<#Readlink>) for more details.

<a name="Root.Remove"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Remove](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L188>)

```go
func (r *Root) Remove(name string) error
```

Remove removes the named file or \(empty\) directory in the root. See [Remove](<#Remove>) for more details.

<a name="Root.RemoveAll"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [RemoveAll](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L194>)

```go
func (r *Root) RemoveAll(name string) error
```

RemoveAll removes the named file or directory and any children that it contains. See [RemoveAll](<#RemoveAll>) for more details.

<a name="Root.Rename"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Rename](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L223>)

```go
func (r *Root) Rename(oldname, newname string) error
```

Rename renames \(moves\) oldname to newname. Both paths are relative to the root. See [Rename](<#Rename>) for more details.

<a name="Root.Stat"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Stat](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L200>)

```go
func (r *Root) Stat(name string) (FileInfo, error)
```

Stat returns a [FileInfo](<#FileInfo>) describing the named file in the root. See [Stat](<#Stat>) for more details.

<a name="Root.Symlink"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [Symlink](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L247>)

```go
func (r *Root) Symlink(oldname, newname string) error
```

Symlink creates newname as a symbolic link to oldname. See [Symlink](<#Symlink>) for more details.

Symlink does not validate oldname, which may reference a location outside the root.

On Windows, a directory link is created if oldname references a directory within the root. Otherwise a file link is created.

<a name="Root.WriteFile"></a>

### func \(\*Root\) [WriteFile](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/root.go#L264>)

```go
func (r *Root) WriteFile(name string, data []byte, perm FileMode) error
```

WriteFile writes data to the named file in the root, creating it if necessary. See [WriteFile](<#WriteFile>) for more details.

<a name="Signal"></a>

## type [Signal](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/exec.go#L233-L236>)

A Signal represents an operating system signal. The usual underlying implementation is operating system\-dependent: on Unix it is syscall.Signal.

```go
type Signal interface {
    String() string
    Signal() // to distinguish from other Stringers
}
```

<a name="Interrupt"></a>The only signal values guaranteed to be present in the os package on all systems are os.Interrupt \(send the process an interrupt\) and os.Kill \(force the process to exit\). On Windows, sending os.Interrupt to a process with os.Process.Signal is not implemented; it will return an error instead of sending a signal.

```go
var (
    Interrupt Signal = syscall.SIGINT
    Kill      Signal = syscall.SIGKILL
)
```

<a name="SyscallError"></a>

## type [SyscallError](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/os/error.go#L49-L52>)

SyscallError records an error from a specific system call.

```go
type SyscallError struct {
    Syscall string
    Err     error
}
```
